description: Goal: make cross-module visibility a first-class compiler2 fact. `ModuleDefined(module)` will continue to mean the module body has been scoped/published, while a new `ModuleInterface(module)` fact will mean the module's exported callable surface is available. Strategy: move compiler2 from stringy `ModuleExport` vectors and ad hoc import/require filtering to a module-owned interface model built around `FunctionId`-backed callable entries, exact obligation tracking with requester provenance, and `Ready|Pending` set selection for `All`/`Except` import and require forms. Signal: local, runtime, and external modules all publish one interface shape; exact `only:` imports/requires can stay lazy through interface obligations; `All`/`Except` wait on `ModuleInterface` instead of `ModuleDefined`; missing-export diagnostics come from interface settlement with real requester spans; compiler2 no longer duplicates export selection logic across callers.
title: Compiler2 module interface facts: module-owned callable visibility and external import resolution
